title: GOV.UK - Building regulations approval
notes: Approval may be needed before constructing or changing buildings.
Competent person scheme work can remove the need for the homeowner to apply separately.
Building regulations approval is different from planning permission; a project may need both.
GOV.UK lists examples including fuse boxes/electrics, plumbing bathrooms, windows and doors, roof coverings, heating systems and extra radiators.
Higher-risk building rules route relevant work to the Building Safety Regulator.

title: GOV.UK - Approved Documents collection
notes: Approved Documents provide guidance on ways to meet the building regulations.
The collection links to individual Approved Documents and the merged approved documents.
Use this as the primary source for Approved Document hubs and update checks.

title: GOV.UK - Approved Document L (2026)
notes: Approved Document L (2026) was published on 24 March 2026 and supports Part L of Schedule 1 to the Building Regulations 2010.
Earlier versions continue to apply to buildings subject to previous regulatory standards.
Batch 2 flags Part L pages as needing version-aware implementation.

title: GOV.UK - Building control approval for higher-risk buildings
notes: BSR approval is needed before starting construction of a new higher-risk building or relevant work to an existing higher-risk building.
The guidance states that competent person scheme work and exempt work do not need building control approval where the project consists only of that work.
For staged applications, work must not start until the relevant stage is approved.

title: GOV.UK - BSR building control approval application data collection
notes: Defines higher-risk buildings in this collection as at least 7 storeys or at least 18 metres high plus relevant use thresholds.
States HSE as BSR became the building control authority for higher-risk buildings in England on 1 October 2023.
Collection last updated 3 June 2026 at time of check.

title: GOV.UK - Building Control Independent Panel report
notes: Useful for editorial framing: building control is a regulatory oversight function, not a guarantee of construction quality.
The report explains the mixed economy of local authority building control and private registered building control approvers for most buildings.
Use only for context pages, not consumer decision rules unless cited carefully.

title: Planning Portal - Building control and planning application procedure
notes: Planning Portal is useful for user journey and application-start links.
GOV.UK remains the primary source for rules; Planning Portal can support practical application guidance.
